human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for registrar-services onboarding and enablement specificity, FERPA-aware records handling, program and learner workflow dependencies, study and IRB escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to registrar-services onboarding, training, credential-readiness, access validation, go-live handoff, and blocked-readiness escalation rather than generic training administration.

Authority sources cover FERPA regulations, Department of Education consent guidance, Federal Student Aid enrollment reporting, AACRAO registrar professional guidance, PESC education-data standards, HLC accreditation criteria, and Common Rule research-governance boundaries.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, access mismatch, go-live without evidence, program and learner workflow handling, study workflow approval blockers, grading or instructional authority refusal, consent evidence gaps, accreditation handoff, low-confidence escalation, conflicting policy escalation, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/higher-education-and-research-institutions/registrar-services-higher-education-specialist/ and remains distinct from registrar official-record, faculty review, accreditation, grants, research compliance, and higher-education compliance lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated registrar-services higher education specialist spec pack for training, enablement, credential-readiness, access validation, go-live handoff, and education-record escalation workflows.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is committed and promoted separately.
